generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PositionEncoderFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder embedded in the first-axis drive of MABI MAX 100 robot, achieving 0.06mm path repeatability for real-time kinematic accuracy. Transmits positional data via Ethernet/IP/PROFINET protocols."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderDataFirstAxis
]

instance CurrentSensorFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor embedded in the first-axis drive of MABI MAX 100 robot, measuring motor current for torque calculation and dynamic behavior monitoring. Integrated with high-speed transmission infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentSensorDataFirstAxis
]

instance GearboxTemperatureSensorFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor monitoring gearbox assembly in the first-axis drive of MABI MAX 100 robot, providing thermal data for wear prediction and operational health assessment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxTemperatureDataFirstAxis
]

instance JointAngleSensorFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution joint angle sensor for the first-axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, providing angular position data critical for trajectory planning and kinematic analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointAngleDataFirstAxis
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Data Transmission
instance PositionEncoderDataFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderFirstAxis
]

instance CurrentSensorDataFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensorFirstAxis
]

instance GearboxTemperatureDataFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxTemperatureSensorFirstAxis
]

instance JointAngleDataFirstAxis : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointAngleSensorFirstAxis
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RawSensorDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of raw sensor data (position, current, temperature, joint angles) from MABI MAX 100 first-axis components, transmitted via MQTT protocol for real-time processing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionEncoderDataFirstAxis, CurrentSensorDataFirstAxis, GearboxTemperatureDataFirstAxis, JointAngleDataFirstAxis
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTDataIngestionEnabler
]

instance OperationalMetricsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Contextual abstraction model transforming raw sensor streams into higher-level operational metrics (efficiency indicators, wear prediction scores) using time-series aggregation algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAbstractionEnabler
]

instance PrognosticAnalyticsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Time-series forecasting model (trained via Simulink/Python) predicting gearbox wear, motor degradation, and operational failures based on historical and real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Logical architecture model (SysML IBD-based) for offline/online optimization of robotic paths to minimize energy consumption or cycle time, integrated with Simulink co-simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PathOptimizationEnabler
]

instance ControlAlgorithmValidationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation-based validation model for closed-loop control algorithms (PID tuning) before deployment to Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller, enabling simulation-to-reality validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlValidationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeDashboardData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed data visualization dataset containing aggregated operational metrics, predictive maintenance alerts, and performance indicators for interactive dashboard presentation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalMetricsModel, PrognosticAnalyticsModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTDataIngestionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based data ingestion framework for continuous streaming of raw sensor data from MABI MAX 100 first-axis components to the Digital Twin system."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeTelemetryService, DataAbstractionService
]

instance DataAbstractionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Contextual data enrichment engine transforming raw sensor streams into operational metrics through time-series aggregation and anomaly detection algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series forecasting engine (Simulink/Python-based) implementing prognostic analytics for gearbox wear prediction and operational failure forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance PathOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Co-simulation environment (Simulink + SysML IBD) for trajectory planning optimization, minimizing energy consumption or cycle time through offline/online parameter tuning."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance ControlValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation-based control algorithm validation platform enabling PID tuning and closed-loop feedback validation before deployment to Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard implementation (SysML-based or web-based) providing real-time monitoring of robot performance metrics and predictive maintenance alerts."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeTelemetryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous collection and aggregation of sensor data (motor torque, gearbox temperature, joint angles) via MQTT streaming for real-time system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTelemetryInsight
]

instance DataAbstractionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregation and contextual enrichment of raw sensor streams into higher-level operational metrics (efficiency indicators, wear prediction scores)."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalMetricsInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard service providing real-time visualization of robot performance metrics aligned with MBSE visualization standards."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceVisualizationInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Prognostic analytics service generating condition-based maintenance alerts for gearbox wear and motor degradation prediction."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceAlertInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-driven parameter optimization service for gear ratios, motor currents, and trajectory planning to enhance robot efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback service enabling simulation-based validation of control algorithms before deployment to Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAlgorithmValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTelemetryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of raw sensor data (position, current, temperature) from MABI MAX 100 first-axis components for real-time system monitoring."
]

instance OperationalMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated operational metrics including efficiency indicators and wear prediction scores derived from contextual data enrichment."
]

instance PerformanceVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard visualization of real-time robot performance metrics aligned with MBSE standards."
]

instance MaintenanceAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance alerts indicating potential gearbox wear or motor degradation based on prognostic analytics."
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of maintenance triggers for stakeholders based on prognostic analytics results."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Parameter optimization recommendations for gear ratios, motor currents, and trajectory planning to enhance robot efficiency."
]

instance ControlAlgorithmValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results for control algorithms tested in simulation environment before deployment to physical system."
]

// C2: Environment Components
instance MabiMax100FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment containing the first-axis components of MABI MAX 100 robot including motor, gearbox, and position sensors."
    base:contains PositionEncoderFirstAxis, CurrentSensorFirstAxis, GearboxTemperatureSensorFirstAxis, JointAngleSensorFirstAxis
]

instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin system environment containing all computational models, data processing components, and service enablers for MABI MAX 100 first-axis."
    base:contains MQTTDataIngestionEnabler, DataAbstractionEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler,
                  PathOptimizationEnabler, ControlValidationEnabler, VisualizationEnabler,
                  RawSensorDataStream, OperationalMetricsModel, PrognosticAnalyticsModel,
                  TrajectoryOptimizationModel, ControlAlgorithmValidationModel,
                  RealTimeDashboardData
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with **real-time, data-driven synchronization** between physical and digital components, where live measurements of the robot's first axis are **streamed via MQTT** for continuous monitoring. The temporal granularity is defined by the **frequency of sensor data acquisition** (e.g., joint angle encoders, torque sensors) and the **latency requirements** of the active components processing this data. While explicit synchronization frequencies are not detailed, the system relies on **low-latency data flows** to maintain a **real-time, data-driven representation** of the physical system's state. The use of **MQTT for live measurements** ensures minimal delay in data transmission, enabling near-instantaneous updates to the digital model. No explicit time scales for services or granularity beyond the streaming protocol are mentioned in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **component-connector architecture**, where the digital shadow is integrated with **active components** (e.g., software modules) to interpret and process incoming data. The system leverages **MQTT for data streaming** between physical sensors (e.g., joint encoders) and the digital model, enabling **real-time synchronization**. The **orchestration** is implicitly defined by the **Simulation components** (e.g., Simulink, other simulation tools) that process the aggregated data. While no explicit **resource management** or **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., state machines, orchestration frameworks) are detailed, the architecture relies on **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., SysML, simulation software) to manage the digital twin's behavior. The **component-connector perspective** (e.g., ACME, AADL) is referenced as a broader methodology, but the specific implementation does not elaborate on these patterns beyond the MQTT-based streaming layer."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements**, **functional architecture**, **logical architecture**, and **product/technical layer**. These stages are governed by methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. Transition management between phases is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, or **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Behavior modeling is further refined using **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed simulations are integrated via tools like **MathWorks Simulink**, other simulation frameworks, or general-purpose languages (e.g., **C++/Java**). Lifecycle governance involves integrating heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives and ensuring interoperability across disparate modeling notations and tools, as referenced in [31]. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010) are mentioned, but the reliance on MBSE methodologies implies adherence to iterative refinement and validation protocols.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system validates model fidelity by **comparing simulated data against measured physical data**, quantifying deviations to assess accuracy. Uncertainty is quantified through **repeatability metrics (e.g., 0.06 mm path repeatability)** and **error detection mechanisms** tied to sensor feedback. Quality assurance processes rely on **direct validation against physical system responses**, ensuring alignment between **digital and physical states**. No mention of **ISO 26262** or **statistical process control (SPC)** is provided in the documents; validation is context-specific to the **manufacturing/control domain** described.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document explicitly mentions **MQTT** as a relevant communication protocol for digital shadows, though it does not extend this to Digital Twins. The **technical network infrastructure** is not detailed beyond the **unidirectional data flow** requirement for digital shadows. No information is provided on **network topology** (e.g., star, mesh), **bandwidth/latency requirements**, **reliability/redundancy mechanisms**, or **security protocols**. The document does not specify **OPC UA, Modbus, or Profibus**, nor does it describe **communication protocols** beyond the implied use of MQTT for data ingestion. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) focuses on data aggregation and abstraction but does not address control or bidirectional communication."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The document does not provide explicit details on **hosting platforms** (cloud/edge/on-premise), **computational/storage requirements**, or **scalability mechanisms**. While it references **SysML** and **Simulink** for modeling and simulation, no mention is made of **containerization technologies** (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes), **deployment models**, or **availability/reliability assurance metrics**. The **Asset Administration Shell** (AAS) is briefly mentioned in the context of digital shadows but does not detail deployment infrastructure. The document does not specify whether the system is deployed on **edge devices**, **cloud servers**, or **on-premise systems**, nor does it describe **high-availability clusters** or **disaster recovery protocols**."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system requires **integration with heterogeneous tools and modeling notations**, including **SysML for system modeling**, **MathWorks Simulink for simulation**, and **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for behavioral descriptions. Data exchange occurs via **MQTT**, a lightweight protocol for **live measurements and streaming**, ensuring interoperability with physical sensors and simulation environments. The system acknowledges dependencies on **external tools** (e.g., simulation software) but does not detail **enterprise system integration** or **standardized interoperability protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, ROS). The integration is primarily **tool-specific**, focusing on **real-time data streaming** rather than broader enterprise or multi-twin interoperability."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The document references **standardized modeling methodologies** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** but does not explicitly outline compliance with **industry-wide standardization frameworks**. Key technical standards implicitly or explicitly mentioned include:
- **Modeling Languages**:
  - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language, ISO/IEC 22324)** for functional/logical architecture modeling (IBDs, state machines).
  - **UML (Unified Modeling Language)** for the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1).
- **Architecture Description Languages**:
  - **ACME (Architecture Description Language for Component Models)**.
  - **AADL (Avionics Application Software Standard)**.
  - **MontiArc** (a model-driven ADL for component-based systems).
- **Simulation Tools**:
  - **MathWorks Simulink** (compliant with **IEC 61499** for function block diagrams, though not explicitly stated).

No mention of **interoperability standards** (e.g., **OPC UA, STEP (ISO/IEC 10303)**), **certification frameworks** (e.g., **ISO/IEC 42010 for systems engineering**), or **Digital Twin-specific standards** (e.g., **IEEE P2414**) is provided. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** is proprietary to the authors and does not align with widely adopted standardization bodies.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **physical safety mechanisms** through the **safety cell** of the robotic arm (e.g., MABI robotic arm, referenced in [44]), which restricts the maximum working range to predefined boundaries, ensuring operator and equipment protection. No explicit **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication, or access control mechanisms) are detailed in the documents, though **MQTT-based sensor streaming** ([52]) is mentioned without specifying security configurations (e.g., TLS, OAuth). The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework ([46], [47], [50]) is referenced as a standard for digital twin integration but does not provide explicit security or privacy governance details. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied through physical constraints (e.g., safety cells) but not through digital or network-level safeguards. **Risk assessment** is not explicitly addressed in the documents, and no **safety-critical system considerations** (e.g., IEC 62443 compliance, functional safety standards) are mentioned beyond physical boundaries."
]
